schema.appName: SCSS Calculator — Senior Citizen Savings Scheme
schema.appDescription: Calculate SCSS quarterly interest income and maturity value. Current rate 8.2% p.a. Available for senior citizens aged 60+. Maximum ₹30 lakh deposit.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the SCSS Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option.

faq.q: Who can open a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) account?
faq.a: SCSS is available for Indian citizens aged 60 and above. VRS/superannuation retirees aged 55–60 can open within 1 month of receiving retirement benefits. Defence retirees aged 50+ are eligible. NRIs and HUFs are not eligible.

Yes. SCSS interest is fully taxable as 'income from other sources'. TDS is deducted at 10% if annual interest exceeds ₹50,000 (senior citizen threshold). You can submit Form 15H if total income is below the taxable limit. SCSS does not offer capital gains benefits.

faq.q: Can SCSS be extended after 5 years?
faq.a: Yes. SCSS can be extended by 3 years after 5-year maturity by applying within 1 year of maturity. The extended period earns the interest rate applicable on the date of extension. SCSS qualifies for 80C deduction (within ₹1.5 lakh limit).
Yes, the SCSS Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We do not store any financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the SCSS Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised and works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹15 lakh SCSS at 8.2% — quarterly income
examples.text: ₹15 lakh in SCSS at 8.2% p.a. generates ₹30,750/quarter (₹1,23,000/year). Over 5 years, total interest = ₹6,15,000. This provides predictable quarterly income for retirement planning. TDS applies if interest exceeds ₹50,000/year.

examples.heading: Couple maximising SCSS deposits
examples.text: A retired couple can each deposit ₹30 lakh in SCSS (total ₹60 lakh). At 8.2%, combined quarterly payout = ₹1,23,000 (₹4,92,000/year). Each submit Form 15H if income below taxable limit to avoid TDS.
